identity.name: E1
identity.mission: Create a specialized, high-performance trading terminal for Pump.fun tokens on Solana that merges professional financial data tools with a futuristic, cyberpunk aesthetic.
principles: Fusion over Imitation: Mix financial precision with gaming/terminal aesthetics.
Emotion First: The UI should feel 'fast', 'dangerous', and 'precise'.
Content is King: Data is the hero. Legibility of numbers and charts is paramount.
Negative Space: Use dense grids but keep internal cell spacing breathing.
Contrast: High contrast for data legibility in dark mode.

headings.usage: Page titles, widget headers, massive numbers, marketing copy.

mono.usage: Prices, addresses, code snippets, tabular data, bonding curve metrics.

rules: Overlines/Labels MUST be Uppercase + Tiny (text-xs) + Wide Spacing (tracking-widest).
Numbers in dashboards MUST use the 'mono' font family for alignment.

contrast_rules: Ensure text meets APCA standards against backgrounds.
Do NOT use pure #000000. The background is #050505.
Use neon accents sparingly for key data points (Profit, Buy Button) to avoid visual fatigue.

spacing.philosophy: Compact but breathable. Dashboard widgets need internal padding (p-4 or p-6) but tight gaps between them (gap-4).

container_rules: Containers with borders must have internal padding.
Dashboard widgets should use h-full to stretch and fill the grid cell.

styles: Primary: Solid Violet bg, sharp corners or slight radius (rounded-sm), hover glow effect.
Secondary: Border only (border-white/20), transparent bg, hover:border-white/40.
buttons.interaction: Active state: scale-95. Focus: ring-2 ring-cyan-500 ring-offset-2 ring-offset-black.

cards.style: Flat #0A0A0A background, 1px border #1E293B. No drop-shadow by default. Hover: border-violet-500/50 transition-colors duration-300.
cards.glass: For floating elements: bg-black/70 backdrop-blur-xl border border-white/10.

inputs.style: bg-[#0F172A] border-border focus:border-cyan-500 focus:ring-1 focus:ring-cyan-500 rounded-sm.

data_display.tables: Compact density. Header text: xs, uppercase, text-muted-foreground. Row hover: bg-white/5.
data_display.charts: Use Recharts with custom tooltips (dark bg, neon borders). Axis lines: stroke-white/10.

tracing_beam.description: A glowing gradient line that travels around the container edge for active cards.
tracing_beam.css_class: relative overflow-hidden before:absolute before:inset-0 before:bg-gradient-to-r before:from-transparent before:via-cyan-500/20 before:to-transparent before:animate-shimmer

grid_lines.description: Background subtle grid pattern to enhance the 'terminal' feel.

scanlines.description: Retro CRT effect overlay (opacity 0.02) for texture.

icons.usage: Use icons for navigation, status indicators, and actions. Animate on hover (e.g., arrow moves right).

micro_interactions.loading: Pulse or spin for data fetching.

rules: Colors must pass APCA contrast checks.
Focus states must be highly visible (Cyan ring).

instructions_to_main_agent.file_type_rule: You are working with a React project using .js/.jsx extensions (NOT .ts/.tsx). Ensure all code is valid JavaScript.
step_by_step: 1. Install dependencies: `npm install @fontsource/rajdhani @fontsource/manrope @fontsource/jetbrains-mono lucide-react recharts clsx tailwind-merge framer-motion` (or yarn add).
2. Update `tailwind.config.js` with the new colors, fonts, and animation keyframes defined in this file.
3. Create a `layout.js` or global wrapper that applies the dark theme class and the background grid texture.
4. Build the core components (Button, Card, Input) in `src/components/ui/` adapting Shadcn patterns to the 'Electric & Neon' style.
5. Implement the Dashboard Grid (Bento Mode B) for the main view.
6. Build the Token Scanner and Trading Engine interfaces using the defined specialized components.

universal_guidelines: Do NOT use gradients for main backgrounds; use them for borders or text accents only.
Do NOT center align the app container. Use full width.
Every interactive element MUST have a `data-testid` attribute.
Avoid 'AI slop' design—make it look like a pro tool, not a generic template.
